methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Bright Machines, a Series C robotics and manufacturing automation company founded by Amar Hanspal and Lior Susan, has raised $300M+ to date. Valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 9 same-sector companies at comparable or adjacent stages, comprising 8 private firms and 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). Key private peers include Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, and Dexterity, valuations spanning $300M to $10.0B.

Two methods triangulate the valuation range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $300M capital raised proxy, implying $510M. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company set yields $1.6B, reflecting the midpoint of the comparable universe. The peer range spans $300M (floor) to $10.0B (ceiling), with notable dispersion driven by stage variance—1X Technologies at Series B commands $10.0B while earlier-stage peers trade lower.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $434M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $1.1B. Key uncertainties include stage alignment within the peer set (1X Technologies' Series B valuation at $10.0B creates upside optionality but limited direct comparability) and revenue/profitability visibility for Bright Machines relative to peers. The wide peer range ($300M–$10.0B) reflects material variation in market positioning and capital efficiency across the robotics sector.
